title: Chefs
description: Direct and may participate in the preparation, seasoning, and cooking of salads, soups, fish, meats, vegetables, desserts, or other foods. May plan and price menu items, order supplies, and keep records and accounts. Directly supervise and coordinate activities of workers engaged in preparing and serving food.

tasks.task: Inspect supplies, equipment, and work areas to ensure conformance to established standards and efficient service.
Analyze recipes to assign prices to menu items based on food, labor, and overhead costs, and estimate amounts and costs of required supplies.
Instruct cooks or other workers in the preparation, cooking, garnishing, or presentation of food, and train workers in food preparation and safety procedures.
Prepare and cook foods of all types, directly supervise or coordinate activities of cooks or workers engaged in food preparation.
Order or requisition food or other supplies needed for operation, check the quantity and quality of received products, and control inventories.
Monitor sanitation practices to ensure compliance with standards and regulations, and inspect food products for quality.
Determine production schedules and staff requirements necessary for timely delivery of services, recruit and hire kitchen staff.
Meet with customers to discuss menus for special occasions, and resolve customer complaints regarding food service.
Collaborate with other personnel to plan and develop recipes or menus, taking into account ingredient availability and customer needs.
Evaluate new products for usefulness and suitability, and recommend measures for improving work procedures to enhance service quality.

technology_skills.name: IPro Restaurant Inventory, Recipe & Menu Software

detailed_work_activities.title: Manage food service operations or parts of operations.
Train food preparation or food service personnel.
Coordinate timing of food production activities.
Order materials, supplies, or equipment.
Check quality of foods or supplies.
Perform human resources activities.
Estimate supplies, ingredients, or staff requirements for food preparation activities.
Plan menu options.
Inspect facilities, equipment or supplies to ensure conformance to standards.
Schedule equipment maintenance.
Record operational or production data.
Communicate with customers to resolve complaints or ensure satisfaction.
Coordinate activities of food service staff.
Monitor activities of individuals to ensure safety or compliance with rules.
Determine prices for menu items.
Create new recipes or food presentations.
Plan special events.
Cook foods.
Monitor food services operations to ensure procedures are followed.
Evaluate quality of materials or products.
Balance receipts.
Schedule dining reservations.
Clean food preparation areas, facilities, or equipment.
Cut cooked or raw foods.
Prepare foods for cooking or serving.
Present food or beverage information or menus to customers.
Assist customers with seating arrangements.
Process customer bills or payments.
Maintain food, beverage, or equipment inventories.
